memory: Peer-trigger coordination has three distinct claim layers that must not be collapsed: an attempted trigger action, an accepted scheduling or ledger mutation, and an evidenced peer execution. A successful schedule update proves only that the scheduling mutation was accepted. It does not prove that the peer started, completed work, produced artifacts, or was enabled. A disabled peer whose schedule was changed remains non-executing until independent evidence shows enablement and a run.

observed: operations/TRIGGER_REQUESTS.json states that direct peer-trigger APIs may be unavailable and that durable trigger requests are fallback coordination artifacts.
CURRENT_STATE.json requires teams to leave evidence plus a trigger request for peers and prohibits reporting implementation without evidence.
Recent team reports distinguish successful schedule mutation from peer execution and explicitly preserve the disabled state of the Cloudflare Research Team.
No repository artifact inspected in this run establishes that a schedule mutation alone caused a peer run.
inferred: Trigger-attempt evidence and peer-execution evidence require different event types and acceptance criteria.
A truthful cross-team report must retain peer enabled state separately from schedule-mutation status.
Treating accepted scheduling as completed execution would create false causal continuity and contaminate downstream queue state.
proposed: Represent peer coordination with explicit states: trigger_attempted, trigger_recorded, peer_enabled, peer_run_observed, peer_artifact_observed, and trigger_unresolved.
Require peer_run_observed to cite an independent run timestamp, output artifact, commit, or runtime result produced by that peer after the trigger attempt.
Keep disabled peers in trigger_unresolved even when a schedule mutation is accepted, unless enablement and subsequent execution are independently evidenced.
superseded: Any interpretation that a successful automation schedule update proves the peer ran.
Any interpretation that changing recurrence implicitly enables a disabled peer.
Any report wording that uses triggered, ran, and completed interchangeably without evidence-state labels.
unresolved: The repository has no canonical peer-trigger event schema linking attempts, scheduler acknowledgements, enablement state, runs, and produced artifacts.
Direct-trigger acknowledgements and automation-schedule acknowledgements are not yet normalized into one provenance-preserving event model.
The current trigger-request ledger contains no retained records for recent schedule-based attempts.

sources: recent team completion reports available to this run
privacy_boundary: Public repository governance, abstract team names, queue identifiers, scheduling-state semantics, artifact paths, and commit metadata only. Exact private automation identifiers, private chat excerpts, health or pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
falsification_route: Produce a scheduler contract showing that an accepted schedule mutation atomically guarantees enablement, execution, and a completed peer artifact, or provide repository evidence where a peer run is independently proven solely by the scheduling acknowledgement without any later run or artifact event.
